varieties.name: Common Horseradish (Maliner Kren)
varieties.traits: Broad, crinkled leaves that are rounded at the base; vigorous growth; traditional heirloom variety
varieties.yield_notes: High root quality but variable yield
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (seasonal), Garden Express, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Mudbrick Herb Cottage (shipped to QLD)

varieties.name: Bohemian Horseradish
varieties.origin: Czech Republic/Central Europe, brought to US by German immigrants 1800s
varieties.traits: Smooth, narrow leaves that taper at the base; more disease resistant than Common variety
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high, more reliable than Common
varieties.availability: Specialty herb suppliers, J.W. Jung Seed Company (US), Scenic Hill Farm Nursery (US ships to Australia), Australian specialty nurseries by request

varieties.name: Big Top Western
varieties.origin: United States, bred in late 20th century (1970s-1980s)
varieties.traits: Wide, smooth leaves with tapering base; bred specifically for disease resistance, especially to turnip mosaic virus

varieties.name: Bohemian Giant
varieties.traits: Smooth leaves, tapered base; vigorous growth
varieties.availability: Bulbs n Blooms (US), specialty herb suppliers

varieties.name: New Bohemian
varieties.origin: Czech Republic, improved selection of traditional Bohemian
varieties.traits: Smooth, narrow leaves; improved disease resistance over original Bohemian
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, may be listed as 'Improved Bohemian' in some catalogs

varieties.name: Sass
varieties.traits: Smooth leaves, heart-shaped base; Bohemian classification Type I
varieties.yield_notes: High yield of large tender roots

varieties.name: Swiss
varieties.traits: Smooth leaves, intermediate base shape; Type II classification
varieties.availability: Limited availability, specialty herb collections

varieties.name: Variegata (Variegated Horseradish)
varieties.traits: Striking variegated leaves with irregular white/cream markings and green; large, wavy leaves; ornamental and edible
varieties.yield_notes: Lower vigor than standard varieties; may take 2-3 years to establish
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Garden Express, specialty ornamental herb suppliers

varieties.name: Czechoslovakian
varieties.traits: Smooth to slightly crinkled leaves; very disease resistant
varieties.availability: Herb Society of America, specialty suppliers, heirloom seed savers

varieties.name: Hot Lava
varieties.traits: Vigorous green foliage
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, online nurseries

varieties.name: Polish Red
varieties.traits: Distinctive feature: reddish-brown skin on roots

varieties.name: Firecracker
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth, robust foliage
varieties.availability: Online specialty nurseries, modern cultivar suppliers

varieties.name: Great White
varieties.traits: Large, bright green leaves; vigorous growth
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, online nurseries

varieties.name: Tetskas
varieties.traits: Traditional cultivar with vigorous growth

varieties.name: East European
varieties.traits: Vigorous perennial growth; crinkled to smooth leaves depending on selection

varieties.name: PKM-1 (Periyakulam 1)
varieties.origin: Periyakulam Horticultural College and Research Institute, Tamil Nadu, India
varieties.traits: Annual moringa hybrid; wide, dark green leaves; bushy habit; rapid growth after harvesting; height 4-6 meters
varieties.yield_notes: 300-400 pods per plant annually; starts flowering within 3-4 months of planting
varieties.availability: Moringa International, Srinivasa Agro Seeds, Farmers Stop, agricultural universities in tropical monsoon climates, local nurseries in tropical monsoon climates and throughout tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: PKM-2 (Periyakulam 2)
varieties.origin: Periyakulam Horticultural College and Research Institute, Tamil Nadu, India (improvement over PKM-1)
varieties.traits: More lateral branching than PKM-1 (desirable for leaf production within reach); improved characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: 48% increase over PKM-1; average 98 tonnes per hectare; soft and delicious when cooked with less fiber
varieties.availability: Moringa International, Srinivasa Agro Seeds, Farmers Stop, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, local agricultural suppliers throughout tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: ODC-3 🌱
varieties.origin: Odisha/Eastern India, released 2017 after six generations of continuous selfing
varieties.traits: Perennial variety; pure line selection; developed through observation of flowering season, fertilizer needs, water requirement, fruit set
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity for both pods and leaves
varieties.availability: Moringa International, Farmers Stop, agricultural research stations, tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Bhagya 🌱
varieties.origin: India, developed for short-duration high-yielding production
varieties.traits: Annual variety; fast-growing; bushy habit
varieties.yield_notes: High yield in short duration; early maturity
varieties.availability: Agricultural universities, local tropical monsoon climates suppliers, seed banks

varieties.name: GKVK-1 🌱
varieties.origin: University of Agricultural Sciences, Bengaluru (Gandhi Krishi Vigyan Kendra), Karnataka, India
varieties.traits: Improved variety; developed specifically for pod and leaf production
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent leaf production; ideal for nutritional purposes
varieties.availability: University of Agricultural Sciences Bengaluru, agricultural extension offices, tropical monsoon climates agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Rohit-1 🌱
varieties.origin: India, developed for high-quality leaf production
varieties.traits: Developed specifically for nutritional leaf harvest; more lateral branching for accessible leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Maximum leaf production; ideal for leaf powder and fresh consumption
varieties.availability: Agricultural research stations, tropical monsoon climates agricultural universities, local suppliers

varieties.name: KDM-1 🌱
varieties.traits: Drought-tolerant; high productivity under rainfed conditions; resilient to water stress
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity despite water limitations; excellent for non-irrigated farming
varieties.availability: Moringa International, agricultural extension services, tropical monsoon climates agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Moringa
varieties.origin: IARI (Indian Agricultural Research Institute), New Delhi, India
varieties.traits: Improved variety; better adaptability to different regions including cooler climates
varieties.availability: IARI, agricultural universities, national seed distributors

varieties.name: Coimbatore-1 🌱
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India
varieties.traits: Perennial variety; known for consistent yield and pod quality

varieties.name: Valayapatti 🌱
varieties.traits: Local variety adapted to South Indian conditions

varieties.name: Narendra Moringa-1
varieties.origin: Narendra Deva University of Agriculture and Technology, Uttar Pradesh, India
varieties.traits: Adaptable to cooler climates; suitable for northern Indian states
varieties.availability: Agricultural universities, national seed networks

varieties.name: MX-3
varieties.traits: Mixed characteristics; adaptable variety

varieties.name: Wingless 🌱
varieties.origin: India, selection for specific seed characteristics
varieties.traits: Seeds without wings; mainly used for food and oil extraction purposes
varieties.yield_notes: Good seed production; valuable for oil and kernel harvest
varieties.availability: Moringa International, specialized moringa seed suppliers in India

varieties.name: Traditional Perennial Moringa 🏆
varieties.origin: India, cultivated for centuries in tropical monsoon climates and throughout India

varieties.name: Moringa stenopetala (African Moringa)
varieties.traits: Grows slower than M. oleifera but has larger leaves that are easier to collect; more drought-tolerant
varieties.yield_notes: Good leaf production; excellent for leaf harvest
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, international moringa seed banks, some tropical monsoon climates botanical gardens
